meta.pageDescription: Step into a student's seat and try the ClassAuto AI tutor in 3 minutes. No sign-up.

hero.subtitle: Watch a real lecture clip and ask the AI tutor anything. No sign-up required.
hero.scrollHint: Pick a field below to start

fieldSelect.title: Which field would you like to try?
fieldSelect.subtitle: We'll instantly show you a 5-minute lecture clip and AI tutor tailored to your choice.

experience.videoPlaceholderDesc: Real clips are being prepared for the beta launch. For now you'll see the still preview frame.
experience.videoCaption: Lecture preview re-synthesized with the AI avatar voice
experience.chatGreeting: Hi! Ask me anything about the lecture.
experience.limitReachedDesc: Want real questions from real students? Build your own lecture.
experience.lastQuestionHint: 💡 One question left. Try something off-topic — see how ClassAuto reacts!

toast.offTopicBody: Type something off-topic like "What's for lunch today?" — see how the AI responds.

answer.offTopicTitle: This question is outside the lecture's scope.
answer.offTopicBody: I couldn't find an answer in the lecture materials. Other EdTech chatbots will answer anything — ClassAuto is designed to keep students focused on learning.

mockAnswers.socialQ1: GDP measures everything produced inside a country, while GNP measures what a country's nationals produce at home and abroad. Since launching the digital yuan, China has improved the accuracy of its GDP statistics.
mockAnswers.socialQ2: The digital yuan is fundamentally different from Bitcoin: it's issued directly by the central bank and every transaction is traceable. The design prioritizes policy visibility over anonymity.
mockAnswers.naturalQ1: The constancy of the speed of light means the speed measured in any inertial frame is always about 299,792,458 m/s. From this single assumption, time dilation and length contraction follow naturally.
mockAnswers.naturalQ2: The twin paradox is a thought experiment in which one twin travels near light speed and returns younger than the twin who stayed home. The asymmetry from the traveling twin's acceleration is the key.

suggested.socialQ1: What is the difference between GDP and GNP?
suggested.socialQ2: How is the digital yuan different from Bitcoin?
suggested.naturalQ1: Why is the speed of light constant?

ctaModal.title: How was the experience?
ctaModal.body: The clip you just watched is a real lecture by Prof. Eoheung — built from a single PPT in about 12 minutes.
ctaModal.primary: 🎓 Apply for the beta and build your own

faq.title: Demo FAQ
faq.q1: Can I really try everything without signing up?
faq.a1: Yes — watching the video and chatting with the AI tutor are both available without an account.
faq.q2: Will my own lecture turn into a video like this?
faq.a2: Yes — a single PPT is all you need.
faq.q3: Is this the same screen students actually see?
faq.a3: It's identical to what students experience.
faq.q4: Can I try both demo videos?
faq.a4: One video per session, but "Try again" lets you switch to the other one.

footerCta.title: Now it's your turn to build one
footerCta.subtitle: The beta is free. With one PPT you can ship your first lecture in under 12 minutes.
